title: KISS transport: response frame buffer too small for worst-case
description:
File: lichen/subsys/lichen/transport/kiss_transport.c
Lines: 262-267

In handle_sethardware(), the response frame buffer is 128 bytes:

    uint8_t resp_frame[128];
    ...
    if (kiss_encode(0, resp_cmd, response, (size_t)resp_len,
                    resp_frame, sizeof(resp_frame), &resp_frame_len) == 0) {

But response buffer is 64 bytes (line 250):
    uint8_t response[64];

With KISS encoding, worst case is 2x + 3 bytes:
- 64 * 2 + 3 = 131 bytes

This exceeds the 128-byte resp_frame buffer. If the hardware callback returns a 64-byte response where every byte needs escaping, kiss_encode() will return -ENOMEM.

While the code handles this gracefully (no response sent), it's a silent failure.

Fix: Increase resp_frame to at least 131 bytes, or reduce response buffer to 62 bytes (62*2+3=127).
notes: Commit: 0361e43a8 - response frame buffer increased to 132 bytes to cover worst-case KISS encoding of 64-byte hardware responses
close_reason: Fixed by increasing resp_frame[132] in handle_sethardware() (already present in codebase); verified KISS encode worst-case fits; committed beads metadata
